event_date: APR 07 "Bill Owens Work and Leisure" Des Moines Art Center Recurring weekly on Sunday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday until April 19, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: Exhibit Runs: December 20, 2025 - April 19, 2026 Bill Owens documented the suburbs in the 1970s, capturing the lives of prosperous, predominantly white Americans at a moment after the cultural shifts of the 1960s but before the dawn of the digital age of computers and video games in the 1980s. His photographs were compiled in the best-selling coffee table books “Leisure” (1972), “Our Kind of People” (1975), and “Working: I Do It For the Money” (1977). The images in this exhibition, taken from all three of these series and shot mainly in California and the Midwest, reveal Owens’ unique comedic eye as he seems to lightly poke fun at his subjects while clearly enjoying their company, finding both the genuine and the absurd in everyday life. The view of America his photography presents is specific and limited to a certain financial, racial, and regional demographic, but in many ways, it also resembles the nostalgic way that era is remembered in pop culture. Now 50 years old, these scenes will look charmingly familiar to many viewers and blasted from the distant past to others. Owens titled his images with quotes from his subjects, allowing their voices to reach us decades later. Through Owens’ lens, they invite us into their homes, share a day at the office, and take us along to several kinds of parties. These photos allow us all to take a moment and join the fun. Bill Owens (American, born 1938) Karate is a highly competitive sport, like tennis or boxing. It’s on one. For $30 a month a kid can get some good mental and physical training. He can learn coordination, balance, timing and self-defense. Livermore, California, from the “Leisure” series, 1974 (printed 1999) Gelatin silver print Des Moines Art Center Permanent Collections: The Jeff Perry Photography Collection given in honor of Myron and Jacqueline Blank, 2024.120

event_title: “Honey, You’re a Wonderful Model: Maria Lassnig’s Animated Films” Exhibit
event_date: APR 07 “Honey, You’re a Wonderful Model: Maria Lassnig’s Animated Films” Exhibit Des Moines Art Center Recurring weekly on Sunday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday until May 17, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: An immersive installation of moving images, “Honey, You’re a Wonderful Model” presents Austrian artist Maria Lassnig’s (1919-2014) hand-drawn animated films from the 1970s. Lassnig’s animations are populated by a cast of zany characters, frequently including Lassnig herself, who engage in physical comedy and slapstick antics while addressing the complexities of relationships, sexuality, grief, artistic creation, and embodiment. The exhibition, which will feature a 16mm projection, a black box theater, and a series of suspended screens, is the artist’s first solo exhibition in the Midwest and the first exhibition in the United States dedicated to her funny and irreverent animations. Viewers will have the opportunity to see Lassnig’s films alongside eleven drawings created in New York in the 1970s and her 1975 painting “Fischbild / Einen Fisch essen (Fish Picture / Eating a Fish),” shown publicly for the first time since it was acquired by a private collection in the 1970s. Lassnig is well known as a painter of unflinching self-portraits, but she also spent a decade in New York working as an experimental and feminist filmmaker. Her animated films are a unique part of her practice in which she was not only overtly silly but also used comedy to interrogate the human experience. Maria Lassnig (Austrian, 1919-2014) / "Selfportrait", 1971 / (film still) / © Maria Lassnig Foundation / Courtesy sixpackfilm https://desmoinesartcenter.org/art/exhibitions/maria-lassnig/ ART CENTER HOURS: Monday Closed Tuesday 10am - 4pm Wednesday 10am - 4pm Thursday 10am - 7pm Friday 10am - 7pm Saturday 10am - 5pm Sunday 10am - 5pm

event_title: “Iowa Artists 2026 - Henry Payer: Aagakinąk Haciwi: We Live Opposite Each Other” On View
event_date: APR 07 “Iowa Artists 2026 - Henry Payer: Aagakinąk Haciwi: We Live Opposite Each Other” On View Des Moines Art Center Recurring weekly on Sunday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday until June 17, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: For the 2026 edition of Iowa Artists, the annual exhibition series celebrating artists from our home state, which has been ongoing since 1950, the Art Center welcomes Sioux City-born artist Henry Payer (born 1986). A member of the Ho Chunk people, whose ancestral territory spans areas in Wisconsin, Minnesota, Illinois, and Iowa, Payer explores his history and identity as an Indigenous American. “Aagakinąk Haciwi: We Live Opposite Each Other” will feature Payer’s signature mixed media works with imagery from both Native traditions and Western popular culture. Friday, January 16, 2026 from 5 - 7 pm join us for the opening celebration. Bring family and friends to view the artwork, and kick off the evening with complimentary lite bites and delicious desserts. Beer and wine available for purchase. Remarks from Kelly Baum, John and Mary Pappajohn Director and CEO, will take place at 6 pm. Henry Payer (Ho Chunk, born 1986) Wandering Winnebago No. 5 Mixed media Courtesy of the artist ART CENTER HOURS: Tuesdays & Wednesdays: 10am - 4pm Thursdays & Fridays: 10am - 7pm Saturdays & Sundays: 10am - 5pm CLOSED MONDAYS

event_date: APR 07 AVID Author Series: Julia Quinn Hoyt Sherman Place QUICK VIEW
description: The 25th anniversary of Authors Visiting in Des Moines (AViD) is upon us! This milestone year features a romance world superstar in Julia Quinn, a literary icon in Tayari Jones, a groundbreaking partnership with the Des Moines Arts Festival and Des Moines Art Center, and much more!. Mark your calendars for these events and get ready for an incredible season of celebrating reading and the arts in Des Moines! JULIA QUINN - Tuesday, April 7: 7:00pm - Hoyt Sherman Place - DOORS OPEN: 5:15pm; seating is first-come, first served! #1 New York Times bestseller Julia Quinn is the author of the wildly popular Bridgerton novels, which were the basis for the Netflix series of the same name. Her books have been translated into 43 languages, and there are over 20 million in print in the United States alone. When not writing, she sits on the board of Landesa, the globally-recognized NGO working to secure land rights for the rural poor. She also served as national ambassador for EveryLibrary and spent eighteen months crisscrossing the country as a spokesperson in the fight against book bans in the United States. A graduate of Harvard & Radcliffe Colleges, she lives in the Pacific Northwest with her family. Admission is free. Doors open at 5:15 PM. Seating is first-come, first-served. Seating is limited; if the venue reaches capacity, we will close seating. At the end of the program, there will be a question and answer period. A book signing will follow. Due to the expected crowd size, we ask that each person bring only one book for Julia to sign. Books will be available to purchase before and after the program courtesy of Beaverdale Books.
address: Historic Sherman Hill, 1501 Woodland Avenue, Des Moines, IA 50309

event_date: APR 09 Church Basement Ladies The Ingersoll QUICK VIEW
description: Dining Time: 5:30pm; Showtime: 7:30pm EVENING DATES: March 26, March 27, March 28, April 2, April 3, April 4, April 9, April 10, April 11 Church Basement Ladies is a warm, laugh-out-loud musical comedy that celebrates friendship, food, and the women who quietly keep a community running. Set in the basement of a rural Minnesota Lutheran church in the early 1960s, the story follows five very different women as they prepare meals, navigate change, and share their hopes, worries, and wisdom. Filled with catchy songs, heartfelt moments, and plenty of humor, Church Basement Ladies is a nostalgic, feel-good show about faith, family, and the bonds that form over coffee, casseroles, and countless potlucks. Each ticket includes a chef-crafted gourmet meal designed to elevate your night from the very first bite. Your dining experience begins with a crisp house salad, followed by warm, freshly baked breads served with creamy butter. Then indulge in your choice from a curated selection of exceptional entrées, each prepared with care and bold flavor: All meals include standard beverage service, with upgrade options available.

What to Expect - A safe, engaging indoor play space for children - A relaxed setting for parents and caregivers to connect - An outdoor playground area called the Discovery Park with towers, music stations and sensory walls - Flexible arrival and departure—come when it works for your family - A great opportunity for families new to Valley or looking to build relationships

event_date: APR 09 Henry Cho Hoyt Sherman Place QUICK VIEW
description: Henry Cho: The Empty Nest Tour on April 9 @ 8:00 pm Tickets on sale starting September 19 @ 10am Reserved Seating: $39.75, $49.75, $59.75 On February 11, 2025, comedian Henry Cho was inducted as the 229th member of The Grand Ole Opry. This prestigious honor has not been extended to a comedian since 1973. Henry’s TV credits include appearances on NBC’s The Tonight Show, CBS’s “The Late, Late, Show,” and NBC’s “Young Comedians Special.” He served two years as host of NBC’s Friday Night Videos and had many guest roles on various network sitcoms. Henry was co-creator, co- producer and co-writer of “The Henry Cho Show” on GAC. Henry’s one hour Comedy Central Special, “What’s That Clickin Noise?” Was also on Netflix. Henry can also be heard daily on Sirius, XM, Blue Collar Radio and Pandora. Some of Henry’s film credits include Universal’s McHale’s Navy with Tom Arnold and David Allen Greer; Say It Isn’t So with Heather Graham and Sally Field; and Material Girls with Hilary Duff and Angelica Houston produced by Madonna. Henry was the keynote entertainer for The 59th Annual Radio & Television Correspondents’ Dinner and has worked extensively with Vince Gill, Amy Grant, Willie Nelson, Carrie Underwood, Reba and many others. Henry’s clean comedy is so versatile that he can headline Vegas and then tour with Michael W. Smith. Henry filmed the Faith-based Indy film “Saving Faith” in which he co-starred and also co-producer was released by Lions Gate. Henry has been nominated for Best Supporting Actor in a Feature Film by the International Christian Film Festival. Henry recently completed filming a Hallmark movie “The Farmer and the Belle- Saving Santaland”. He’ll start shooting a TV pilot, “Double Occupancy” early July and also the film, “Cheyenne”. He’s working on his own project with Triple Horse Studios, the guys behind “Case for Christ”.
address: Historic Sherman Hill, 1501 Woodland Avenue, Des Moines, IA 50309

event_date: APR 11 “Christine Rebet: Sound of Time” Des Moines Art Center Recurring weekly on Sunday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday until August 26, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: On view April 11 - August 23: see website for daily hours! This survey exhibition presents five films made between 2015 and 2025 by Paris-based artist Christine Rebet, which play consecutively in the Pamela Bass-Bookey and Harry Bookey Gallery. Rebet has devoted her practice to the act of drawing, creating lush and labor-intensive hand-drawn animations marked by vivid color and lively images that change and morph alongside lyrical voice-over narrations. “I am interested in preserving hand-drawn animation for myself because it is my act, my gesture,” Rebet has explained. “My gesture is to draw. The magic of the hand.” In her films, Rebet imagines and invents visual forms for forgotten rituals, sacred landscapes, and oral histories. Her works are born out of travel and research in places as diverse as Jamaica, the Persian Gulf, Thailand, Southern Iraq, and Haiti, locales where she meets and collaborates with local experts—including musicians, monks, and archaeologists—bringing their knowledge to life through the medium of drawing. The films put historical fact in tension with objects, ideas, and traditions whose forms are unknown, emphasizing the slippery and enigmatic aspects of human knowledge and history. Christine Rebet (French, born 1971) The Fall, 2025 Animation shot on HD, sound 5:19 minutes Courtesy of the artist and Bureau, New York

event_date: APR 11 Social Chess Club Rook Room Game Lounge & Cafe Recurring monthly on the 2nd Saturday until September 12, 2026 QUICK VIEW
description: Drop by the Rook Room Game Lounge & Cafe on Saturday, October 11th, and get strategic with our monthly Social Chess Club. Social Chess Club meets every second Saturday of the month from 8 AM to Noon at the Rook Room Game Lounge & Cafe. We have chessboards and clocks available, or feel free to bring your own. This is a for-fun social club designed to build a community of chess players, both new to the game and long-time players. This is not organized tournament play. We encourage attendees to engage in friendly competition, introduce new players to the game, and help spread the love of chess. Food and beverages – including our breakfast and lunch menus, cafe menu, and drinks menu – will be available for ordering throughout. So come hungry, thirsty, and ready to play. Registration is not required, and players can come and go as they please. This is a free-to-attend event. All ages and skill levels are encouraged to join in the fun. Please note that this is a loosely structured event hosted at and by the Rook Room. There is no dedicated staff member available to help learn and play. We encourage attendees to support each other in learning and playing the game!

event_date: APR 11 Church Basement Ladies The Ingersoll QUICK VIEW
description: Dining Time: 11:00am; Showtime: 1:00pm DAYTIME DATES: March 28, March 29, April 4, April 5, April 11 & April 12 Church Basement Ladies is a warm, laugh-out-loud musical comedy that celebrates friendship, food, and the women who quietly keep a community running. Set in the basement of a rural Minnesota Lutheran church in the early 1960s, the story follows five very different women as they prepare meals, navigate change, and share their hopes, worries, and wisdom. Filled with catchy songs, heartfelt moments, and plenty of humor, Church Basement Ladies is a nostalgic, feel-good show about faith, family, and the bonds that form over coffee, casseroles, and countless potlucks. Each ticket includes a chef-crafted gourmet meal designed to elevate your night from the very first bite. Your dining experience begins with a crisp house salad, followed by warm, freshly baked breads served with creamy butter. Then indulge in your choice from a curated selection of exceptional entrées, each prepared with care and bold flavor: All meals include standard beverage service, with upgrade options available.

event_date: APR 11 Des Moines Laitno Film Festival Varsity Cinemas QUICK VIEW
description: The festival brings award-winning films from Latin America and the U.S.! This year includes American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez, a Sundance Festival Favorite starring Edward James Olmos, about the playwright and director behind Zoot Suit and La Bamba. And A Poet, a Cannes Jury Prize winner from Colombia, about a broken writer in Medellín who finds unexpected redemption through mentorship, and many more! Beyond the films, there's a larger story here: what it means for a Latino family in Des Moines to sit in a theater and watch a film that reflects their world. The films vary in length, themes, and genres. Some are for all ages, some are better suited for mature audiences. The festival is made possible by sponsors. There are coupons available on the website to make it affordable and accessible to all.

event_title: Live Performance, Film Screening, and Artist Conversation: “The Fall”
event_date: APR 11 Live Performance, Film Screening, and Artist Conversation: “The Fall” Des Moines Art Center QUICK VIEW
description: Christine Rebet, the 2026 Toni and Tim Urban Artist-in-Residence, has an exhibition on view at the Des Moines Art Center, “Christine Rebet: Sound of Time,” April 11 – August 23 in the Pamela Bass-Bookey and Harry Bookey Gallery. During this special live artistic performance, visitors are invited to experience “The Fall” featuring Simone Harris as Lady Blake Ophelia Stratum, followed by a screening of Rebet’s film “The Fall” (2025). After the screening, sit in for a dialogue between Harris and Rebet, moderated by Art Center Associate Curator Ashton Cooper. In the early 18th century, in the blue mountains of Jamaica, enslaved Africans who had escaped from plantations founded autonomous communities of Maroons. Queen Nanny, their supreme leader, led their struggle for freedom. She is one of the most celebrated figures from Jamaica and embodies the victorious fight against slavery and colonialism, as well as the resistance of the island’s women. Her spirit remains very much alive. The Windward Maroons, from the community of Moore Town, celebrate their Queen every year in dance ceremonies. Simone Harris, one of the Queen’s descendants, revisits the history of this legendary figure in her performance. Free; registration required. Image: Simone Harris as Lady Blake. Photo by Kevin Tschierse.

event_date: APR 11 Matt McCusker Hoyt Sherman Place QUICK VIEW
description: Matt McCusker: Healing Frequency Tour April 11, 2026 | 7:00 pm On Sale: November 21, 2025 at 10:00 AM Reserved Seating: $35-$45 Matt McCusker is a comedian, novelist, and actor from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. Affectionately known as “The Shaman,” Matt is the co-host of the industry-changing cult comedy show Matt and Shane’s Secret Podcast with Shane Gillis. The show is the number one Patreon podcast in the world, dominating the charts with over 5.5 million downloads each month and consistently ranking in the Top 15 on Spotify’s Comedy Charts. The duo recently announced a major partnership with Spotify to bring video episodes to the platform, marking an exciting new chapter for their devoted audience. McCusker’s highly-anticipated debut Netflix special, A Humble Offering, premiered in October 2025, quickly hitting the Top 10. In it, the comedian discussed the homelessness crisis, opined on race relations, and supplied best practices for seducing his wife. In 2023, McCusker released his first ever special, The Speed of Light. Blending sharp wit with McCusker’s trademark grounded resonance, the hour’s take on masculinity, wellness, and relationships quickly amassed almost 4M views on YouTube alone. As an actor, McCusker has played scene-stealing roles in series including the record-breaking Netflix sitcom Tires and the hit web series Gilly & Keeves. In August 2025, McCusker launched his first animated project with the debut short, The Papa John Paradox. Written, produced, and starring McCusker, five shorts in the series have so far been released, directed by Big Mouth‘s Frank Gidlewski. McCusker is also a respected author. His debut novel, Overlook: A Story About Drugs, Disappointment, and the American Dream, a satirical and unflinching look at a blue-collar community in the throes of a missing person case, was received with acclaim. McCusker is in development on his next book project.

We're thrilled to announce the launch of our rec leagues starting the week of March 8th! Join us on Sundays at 3 PM, Wednesdays at 6pm, or Thursdays at 6 PM League runs 6 Weeks for just $15 per week. We also have a selection of Popdarts available for purchase. Feel free to drop by and check them out. For any questions, please reach out to us at pop@ironsideaxeclub.com! event_title: Umeri Concert - Loves Me, Loves Me Not
event_date: APR 12 Umeri Concert - Loves Me, Loves Me Not Sheslow Auditorium at Drake University QUICK VIEW
description: Umeri, the Drake University Alumni Choir, announced its final concert of the 2025-2026 Season. Loves Me, Loves Me Not will take place on Sunday, April 12 at 6 p.m. at Sheslow Auditorium on the Drake campus. The concert will feature Johannes Brahms’ Liebeslieder (Love Song) Waltzes, a collection of more than a dozen pithy pieces about love. They will be paired with other works that address various aspects of love, from the Renaissance to the 21st Century, written by American and British composers. The music features an almost head-spinning variety of musical sounds and styles, from lushly romantic to edgy. There’s even a piece in hoe-down style! The Liebeslieder Waltzes are delightfully varied and are scored for choir and piano four-hands accompaniment, played by Susan Ihnen and Ling-Yu Hsiao. Dr. Jonathan Sturm, concertmaster of the Des Moines Symphony, will be the featured violin soloist for “Love Is,” a piece that summarizes the themes at the heart of this concert.
